title: Supreme Court Rules Right to Walk on Footpaths is a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on footpaths constitutes a fundamental right under the Constitution. The judgment reinforces the broader interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) to include the right to safe pedestrian access in public spaces. This ruling has significant implications for urban planning, encroachment on footpaths, and state obligations to maintain pedestrian infrastructure.
exam_angle: Connects to the expansive judicial interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty); relevant for questions on fundamental rights, judicial activism, and urban governance.
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court of India
Right recognised: Right to walk on footpaths as a fundamental right
Constitutional provision: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Significance: Expands judicial interpretation of right to life to include safe pedestrian access

title: India in Talks to Sell Supersonic BrahMos Missile to UAE
summary: India is in active negotiations to sell its supersonic BrahMos cruise missile to the United Arab Emirates (UAE), according to sources cited by Reuters and News18. BrahMos is a joint venture between India's DRDO and Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya, capable of speeds up to Mach 2.8–3.0. If concluded, this would mark another significant export deal for BrahMos following the Philippines contract signed in 2022, boosting India's defence export ambitions.
exam_angle: Relevant for India's defence exports policy, Make in India in defence, and India-UAE strategic partnership; BrahMos is a key example of joint defence manufacturing under DRDO.
key_facts: Missile: BrahMos supersonic cruise missile
Joint venture: India's DRDO and Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya
Prospective buyer: UAE
Previous export: Philippines (contract 2022)

title: NSA Ajit Doval Holds Talks with Iran's Top Security Official Amid Middle East Tensions
summary: National Security Adviser Ajit Doval met with Iran's top security official to discuss bilateral security concerns amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. The talks signal India's active diplomatic engagement with Iran on regional stability. India maintains strategic interests in Iran including the Chabahar Port project, energy imports, and connectivity to Central Asia.
exam_angle: Relevant for India-Iran bilateral relations, India's West Asia policy, and the significance of Chabahar Port for India's connectivity ambitions; also tests knowledge of the role of NSA in Indian foreign policy.
key_facts: Indian official: NSA Ajit Doval
Counterpart: Iran's top security official
Context: Middle East regional tensions
India's strategic interests in Iran: Chabahar Port, energy, Central Asia connectivity

title: India's Core Sector Growth Slows to Seven-Month Low of 0.5% in May 2026
summary: India's eight core infrastructure sectors recorded a growth rate of just 0.5% in May 2026, the slowest pace in seven months, according to data reported by Business Standard. The eight core sectors — coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement, and electricity — account for about 40.27% of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP). The slowdown raises concerns about the momentum of industrial recovery.
exam_angle: Core sector data is a leading indicator for IIP and GDP; important for GS Paper 3 questions on economic indicators, industrial policy, and macroeconomic performance.
key_facts: Core sector growth (May 2026): 0.5% — a seven-month low
Eight core sectors weight in IIP: ~40.27%
Eight sectors: coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement, electricity
Source: Government of India data reported by Business Standard

title: India Becomes World's Top Ship Recycling Nation in 2025, Achieves Vision 2030 Target Early
summary: India has become the world's leading ship recycling nation in 2025, achieving its Vision 2030 target five years ahead of schedule, according to a Mint report. The Alang ship-breaking yard in Gujarat is the hub of India's ship recycling industry and is one of the largest in the world. This milestone reinforces India's position in the global maritime economy and the circular economy.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 topics on India's maritime sector, blue economy, circular economy, and industrial milestones; Alang (Gujarat) is a frequently asked 'places in news' topic.
key_facts: Achievement: India becomes world's No. 1 ship recycling nation in 2025
Target achieved: Vision 2030 goal, five years ahead of schedule
Key location: Alang ship-breaking yard, Gujarat
Significance: Boosts blue economy and circular economy goals

title: India Seeks Preferential Market Access in Trade Deal Negotiations with US
summary: India's Trade Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India is actively seeking preferential market access as part of ongoing trade deal negotiations with the United States. Goyal expressed urgency, saying the deal should be concluded as fast as possible to give India a competitive advantage. The India-US bilateral trade relationship is valued at over $190 billion annually, and a trade deal has been under negotiation amid US tariff pressures.
exam_angle: Critical for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) questions on India-US trade relations, WTO-compatible preferential trade agreements, and India's export competitiveness.
key_facts: Indian official: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal
Objective: Preferential market access from the US
India-US bilateral trade value: over $190 billion annually
Context: Ongoing India-US trade deal negotiations amid US tariff regime

title: West Bengal's First BJP Government Budget: 20% DA Hike, 1 Lakh Jobs Announced
summary: The BJP government in West Bengal presented its first state budget, announcing a 20% hike in Dearness Allowance (DA) bringing it to 38%, creation of 1.7 lakh jobs, and an increase in MLA Local Area Development funds by Rs 30 lakh. This is historically significant as the BJP won the West Bengal assembly elections and formed the government, ending the TMC's long tenure. The budget reflects the new government's priorities in welfare and employment.
exam_angle: Relevant for Indian polity (state budgets, DA, MPLADS/MLALADS-type funds), federal finance, and 'current events' questions on state government policies.
key_facts: State: West Bengal
Government: First budget of BJP government in West Bengal
DA hike: 20% increase, bringing total DA to 38%
Jobs announced: 1.7 lakh

title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns; Britain to Get Its Seventh PM in a Decade
summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, making him the sixth British PM to leave office prematurely in the last ten years. His exit follows a turbulent tenure marked by political and economic pressures. The UK will now undergo a leadership transition, with candidates from the Labour Party including Andy Burnham, Angela Rayner, and Shabana Mahmood being considered as potential successors.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) questions on UK political system, Westminster model of government, and India-UK bilateral relations (especially the ongoing FTA negotiations).
key_facts: Resigned PM: Keir Starmer (Labour Party)
Significance: Sixth UK PM to resign prematurely in 10 years
Prospective successors: Andy Burnham, Angela Rayner, Shabana Mahmood
Relevance to India: Ongoing India-UK Free Trade Agreement negotiations

title: Israeli Defence Delegation Visits India to Deepen Strategic Partnership
summary: An Israeli defence delegation visited India to strengthen bilateral strategic and defence cooperation. India and Israel have a robust defence partnership, with Israel being one of India's top defence suppliers alongside Russia and the United States. Key areas of cooperation include drones (UAVs), missile defence systems, cybersecurity, and intelligence sharing.
exam_angle: Relevant for India-Israel bilateral relations, India's defence procurement policy, and India's multi-alignment foreign policy; Israel is a key defence technology partner for India.
key_facts: Visiting entity: Israeli defence delegation
Purpose: Deepening India-Israel strategic and defence partnership
Key cooperation areas: drones/UAVs, missile defence, cybersecurity, intelligence
Israel's rank: One of India's top three defence suppliers

title: Kerala High Court Upholds Free Bus Travel Scheme for Women and Transgender Persons
summary: The Kerala High Court has upheld the state government's scheme providing free bus travel to women and transgender persons in Kerala State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) and private buses. The judgment reinforces the state's power to implement affirmative action and welfare measures for marginalised groups. The scheme is part of Kerala's broader social welfare policies aimed at inclusion and gender equity.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) on state welfare schemes, affirmative action, transgender rights post the NALSA judgment, and judicial review of executive policies.
key_facts: Court: Kerala High Court
Scheme: Free bus travel for women and transgender persons
Operator: KSRTC and private buses
Significance: Upholds state welfare/affirmative action; connects to NALSA v. Union of India (2014) on transgender rights

title: Supreme Court Told: CBSE Framed New Class 12 Assessment Policy for Students in Gulf
summary: The Supreme Court was informed that CBSE has framed a new assessment policy specifically for Class 12 students based in Gulf countries. This follows concerns raised about the evaluation framework for Indian students studying abroad who appear for CBSE board exams. The development highlights the regulatory reach of CBSE for Indian diaspora students and the court's supervisory role in education policy.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 on governance, education policy, CBSE's regulatory mandate, and the Supreme Court's role in oversight of public bodies.
key_facts: Body: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)
Policy: New Class 12 assessment framework for students in Gulf countries
Forum: Informed to the Supreme Court of India
Significance: Extends CBSE's regulatory mandate to Indian diaspora students abroad

title: Plea in Supreme Court Seeks CBI Probe into Alleged Ram Temple Fund Irregularities
summary: A petition has been filed in the Supreme Court seeking registration of an FIR and a CBI-led investigation into alleged financial irregularities in the funds collected for the Ram Temple construction in Ayodhya. The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teertha Kshetra Trust manages the temple funds. The plea raises questions of financial accountability and transparency in religious trusts of national significance.
exam_angle: Relevant for polity questions on PIL jurisdiction of the Supreme Court, accountability of public/religious trusts, CBI's investigative mandate, and the Ram Temple issue.
key_facts: Forum: Supreme Court of India (PIL/plea)
Relief sought: FIR registration and CBI-led probe
Subject: Alleged financial irregularities in Ram Temple funds
Trust involved: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teertha Kshetra Trust

title: A New Chapter in India's Nuclear Journey — PIB
summary: The Press Information Bureau (PIB) published a piece marking a new milestone in India's nuclear energy programme. India's nuclear sector is governed by the Atomic Energy Commission and the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE), with the Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) operating reactors. India follows a three-stage nuclear power programme designed by Homi Bhabha, aiming to achieve energy security using thorium reserves.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Energy) on India's nuclear programme, three-stage nuclear doctrine, DAE, NPCIL, and India's energy security strategy.
key_facts: Source: Press Information Bureau (PIB) — official government communication
Governing body: Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) / Atomic Energy Commission
Operator: Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL)
India's nuclear doctrine: Three-stage programme (conceptualised by Homi Bhabha) leveraging thorium reserves

title: Five Eyes Alliance Warns AI Models Could Destabilise Governments Within Months
summary: In a rare joint statement, the Five Eyes intelligence alliance (USA, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand) warned that advanced AI models could be capable of destabilising governments and businesses within months. The statement highlights growing concerns about AI-enabled cyberattacks, disinformation, and autonomous decision-making risks. This marks one of the first multilateral intelligence-level warnings specifically targeting AI safety risks at a geopolitical scale.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Internal Security) on AI governance, cybersecurity threats, and multilateral frameworks for emerging technologies; also relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) on intelligence alliances.
key_facts: Alliance: Five Eyes (USA, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand)
Warning: AI models could destabilise governments and businesses within months
Nature: Rare joint intelligence statement on AI safety risks
Key concerns: AI-enabled cyberattacks, disinformation, autonomous risks

title: ICC Announces Landmark Maternity Policy: Motherhood and Cricket Can Coexist
summary: The International Cricket Council (ICC) has introduced a landmark maternity policy, affirming that professional cricketers can take maternity leave without jeopardising their contracts or international standing. The policy is seen as a major step towards gender equity in cricket, allowing women players to return to the sport post-childbirth without penalty. The move follows similar initiatives in other sports and is expected to encourage more women to pursue professional cricket careers.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 1 (Social Issues)/GS Paper 2 (Governance) on gender equity, women's empowerment in sports, and institutional policy reforms; ICC is an important international sports governance body.
key_facts: Body: International Cricket Council (ICC)
Policy: Landmark maternity policy for women cricketers
Key provision: Maternity leave without penalty to contracts or international status
Significance: Advances gender equity in professional sports

title: CRED Founder Kunal Shah Appointed as Head of WhatsApp by Meta
summary: Meta has appointed Kunal Shah, the Indian entrepreneur and founder of fintech startup CRED, as the new Chief Executive of WhatsApp. Meta simultaneously announced an investment of $900 million in CRED. Kunal Shah becomes one of the most prominent Indian founders to lead a global technology platform. WhatsApp has over 2 billion users worldwide and is particularly dominant in India, its largest market.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech) on the digital economy, Indian startup ecosystem, and Big Tech governance; also a 'persons in news' item for Prelims.
key_facts: Appointed: Kunal Shah (founder of CRED) as head of WhatsApp
Appointing company: Meta
Meta's investment in CRED: $900 million
WhatsApp user base: Over 2 billion globally; India is its largest market

title: Lucknow Coaching Centre Fire: 14 Dead, High-Level Enquiry Ordered
summary: A devastating fire broke out at a coaching centre building in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, killing at least 14 students, with some jumping from the first floor to escape. Uttar Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister ordered a high-level enquiry into the incident. The tragedy highlights recurring concerns about fire safety norms, building code violations, and regulatory oversight of coaching institutes in India.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (Governance) on disaster management, fire safety regulation, and accountability of coaching institutes; also connects to NDMA guidelines and state disaster response.
key_facts: Location: Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh
Casualties: At least 14 dead
Incident: Fire at a coaching centre building
Action taken: High-level enquiry ordered by UP Deputy Chief Minister

title: US Authorises Iranian Oil Sales Amid Ongoing Nuclear Deal Talks
summary: The United States has authorised limited Iranian oil sales as negotiations continue on a final nuclear peace deal with Iran. This marks a significant diplomatic development, as US sanctions on Iranian oil have been a cornerstone of the maximum pressure campaign. The move could impact global oil prices and India's energy import strategy, given India has historically imported Iranian crude oil.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) on Iran nuclear deal (JCPOA revival), India-Iran-US triangular dynamics, and GS Paper 3 (Economy) on global crude oil markets and India's energy security.
key_facts: Action: US authorises limited Iranian oil sales
Context: Ongoing US-Iran nuclear deal negotiations
Historical background: JCPOA (Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action) and US sanctions regime
India angle: India is a major importer of Iranian crude; sanctions have disrupted this trade
